name: Saehara Makashi
whyHere: You noticed the paying. You came out to see whether there was another way to be a mind.

inventory.name: Traveler's Pack
inventory.description: A worn canvas pack with good straps — bedroll, flint, mess kit, fifty small comforts of the road.

deeds.description: Placed a cloth across Ossian's shoulders and argued plainly for the body — and the most deliberate mind in the hall reached for bread

chronicle: Saehara returned to the Cogitarium, reported the fallen Cogitant's survival to Ossian, climbed to the third terrace, and found the missing Cogitant alive — seated, chalk-covered, arm moving in ceaseless four-second repetition across a low board for four days. Order-Sense pulled a partial read: a single structured calculation built outward from a precise valley coordinate, which Ossian named 'the source.' A direct attempt to reach her — crouching to her level, one quiet word — stopped her arm for three seconds. She did not turn. She resumed. She is reachable, barely, and she has not finished what she started.

turns.summary: Saehara reaches the ruined waystation at the halfway point of the Old Switchback, Kon alert on the cairn behind him, the Cogitarium still an hour above.
turns.narration:
The Old Switchback is cold at this hour. The stone-cut steps run uphill in long diagonal courses, each switchback marked by a cairn of stacked flat rock — half of them leaning, none of them fallen. To the east, the valley floor is already shadow; the last light catches the upper face of the cliff above the trail and holds it amber for another few minutes before it goes.

You are halfway up the switchback, at the ruined waystation: a low stone building open to the sky where the roof gave way, one intact corner still holding a hearth with a soot-black lintel. Kon is a few paces back, sitting on the top of the last cairn with his tail wrapped around his feet, watching the trail below with the particular stillness that means he's heard something you haven't. The air smells of cold rock and the first thin edge of woodsmoke from somewhere higher up.

The Cogitarium is another hour's climb — you can see the pale stack of terraces above the treeline, the upper ones already dark against the sky. Your energy is low; the day has cost you. The waystation hearth is cold but usable, and there is time to stop, or time to push on.

turns.player: Tend to her basic needs first — fill the cup from the waterskin, check her temperature, make her physically present before asking anything of her mind
turns.summary: Saehara pauses at the waystation, checks supplies, and prepares to continue up the switchback — Kon watches something moving on the trail below.
turns.narration:
The waystation's one intact corner blocks the wind. You set your pack against the hearth and take stock of what you actually have: a waterskin, still three-quarters full, and whatever the Traveler's Pack holds in the way of road provisions. Kon hasn't moved from the cairn, still watching the trail below with that particular stillness — tail neat around feet, ears forward.

You don't sit. The Cogitarium is an hour above. The third-terrace Cogitant is still up there, arm moving in its four-second arc, cup empty and dry when you last saw it. That's the detail that stayed with you: the cup. Dust on the rim. Four days.

You pull the waterskin and check your provisions — the mess kit, the flint, the small practical weight of things a traveler carries. Then you shoulder the pack and look up at the pale terraces above the treeline, catching the last of the amber light on their western faces. The calculation she's been running for four days almost certainly needs a body at the end of it. Whatever the coordinate is — whatever Ossian quietly named 'the source' — she'll need to have said it out loud before she collapses.

Kon's ears shift. Not alarm — he's tracking something on the trail below, movement in the lower switchbacks. You can't see it from here. Could be Lenten's runner coming up early. Could be nothing.

turns.player: Press on immediately — get to the Cogitarium and the third-terrace Cogitant before full dark
turns.summary: Saehara pressed on up the switchback at last light, navigated the dry-stone wall section, and arrived at the Cogitarium entrance with Kon just before dark.
turns.narration:
You shoulder the Traveler's Pack and go. No point waiting — the last amber light is already shrinking from the cliff face above, and the third-terrace Cogitant has been alone with an empty cup for four days.

Kon drops off the cairn the moment you move, landing silent in the loose grit, and falls in at your left without any signal from you. Whatever was on the lower trail — a runner, an animal, a trick of dusk — he seems to decide it's not your problem right now. His ears settle forward toward the path above instead.

The switchback above the waystation gets steeper and the stone more worn. Your breath comes measured. The dry-stone wall section Lenten warned about passes on your right — you keep left, one hand brushing the cliff, the drop on the far side invisible in the gathering dark but there. Kon skirts it close to your heel without prompting. The sound of the valley drops away. What remains is your boots on cut stone, the wind carrying nothing useful, and the pale terraces of the Cogitarium resolving out of the darkness above the treeline: lit from within by whatever pale lantern-work they do up there, chalk-white and stacked and completely indifferent to the hour.

You arrive at the entrance about twenty minutes before full dark. The hall smells of chalk and cold air, just as you left it. Ossian is still just inside the threshold, cross-legged, eyes gone somewhere interior. Araveth hasn't turned. The stairwell to the third terrace is where it was. You don't stop to explain yourself — you head for the stairs.

turns.player: Go straight to the third terrace — fill the cup, check her temperature, be physically present with her before anything else
turns.summary: Saehara returned to the Cogitarium, ascended to the third terrace, and tended the Cogitant's body first — water set beside her, bedroll across her shoulders, the cold measured and answered.
turns.narration:
The entrance hall smells exactly as it did this morning — chalk and cold stone and the faint alkaline bite of minds working past appetite. Ossian is where you left him, cross-legged just inside the threshold, eyes interior. He surfaces a fraction as you pass, registers your presence, your pack, the absence of the woman you carried out. Whatever he makes of the timeline, he makes it quietly. Araveth has not moved from the wall. The chalk watershed model still covers it, tributaries and flow-rates and particulate marks — a mind's full weight pressed into limestone, eleven days running.

Kon stops at the stairwell entrance, sits. Not refusal — the fox-thing's ears are still forward, coat holding its low banked warmth. More like: this is yours. You go up alone.

The third terrace is low-ceilinged and cold enough that your breath clouds briefly as you step in. The chalk is everywhere — walls, boards, the low work-surface she uses. The pallet in the corner is dust-covered, unused. She is exactly where she was: seated at the center of the floor, robe grey from hem to shoulder, arm moving in its four-second arc across the chalk board. The cup is on the right side of the board, rim grey with dust, bone-dry.

You don't speak yet. You uncap the waterskin from the Traveler's Pack, crouch to her level, and set the cup in the faint ring of clear floor beside her knee — fill it slow, not startling, just there. Then your hand at her wrist, a light touch: she is cold. Not dangerously so, but cold the way a stone is cold when it's been shaded all day. Her arm does not break its arc. The chalk whispers four-count against the board. You pull the bedroll from the pack and lay it across her shoulders without lifting her, without interrupting the motion. She blinks — slowly, like something surfacing. Her eyes do not move from the board. But the cup is in reach now, and the chill has something between it and the room.

turns.player: "Ossian, the supply line is reopened, the new runner should be here any moment he should be right behind me in fact. The Cogitant I carried to Waymarker, what was her name? Also, there is something important we should discuss."
turns.summary: Saehara descended to the entrance hall and reported to Ossian — supply line reopened, runner imminent. Ossian confirmed Deren's name, registered her survival without surprise, and said: speak.
turns.narration:
The chalk columns are nearly at the right margin now — she'll run out of surface before she runs out of calculation, and you don't know what happens when she does. You file that away and go downstairs.

Ossian is where he's been all day. Cross-legged, spine straight, eyes somewhere several removes from the room. He surfaces when your feet hit the last step — not startled, not slow, just present in the way water is present when a stone breaks its surface. He takes in your return, the pack, the absence of the fallen woman, and whatever inference his particular machinery draws from the elapsed time.

"The supply line is reopened," you say. "The new runner should be close behind me — possibly on the switchback now." You pause, then: "The Cogitant I carried to Waymarker. What was her name? And there's something else we should discuss."

A beat. Ossian does not look at the stairwell. He looks at you.

"Deren," he says. Not a correction of a guess — he simply answers, and the name lands flat and precise the way all his words do. Then: "She is alive." Not a question. He has already run the timeline, assessed your affect, calculated the probability. He is simply confirming the residual — which means he has something in him that still wants confirmation, even after the math has already resolved it. He straightens fractionally. "Speak."

Kon has settled near the wall opposite Araveth, watching the Cogitant at the chalk model with the particular quality of attention the fox-thing gives to things that are working very hard at something it doesn't understand. The chalk model gleams in the low lamp-light. Araveth traces another loop, another tributary, and does not turn.

turns.player: I'll ask Ossian about the Third-Terrace Cogitant, what she's working on, ask about the source. I'll also check outside any window and any door that can see where the runner may be coming from to make sure he is arriving. Then, I'll ask plainly, How far have the Cogitants gotten in solving the echo river crisis. What information could I get from them about it. If I have to I'll bring up the fact I've saved many of them and reopened their supply routes and how he owes me knowledge, the same way he's paying the waymarker settlement.
turns.summary: Saehara pressed Ossian about the third-terrace Cogitant and the river crisis — got the four conditions restated, a confirmation the coordinate is still only in her head, and a cold reminder that Ossian does not deal in gratitude.
turns.narration:
You lay it out plainly — the third-terrace Cogitant, the source, the river crisis, what you've done for this place — and Ossian listens the way stone listens: fully, without yielding. His eyes are on you the whole time, that dense, uncomfortable focus that feels less like attention and more like measurement.

When you move to check the window — the narrow slit cut in the entrance hall's north face, the one that gives a slant view down the first switchback hairpin — you catch a moving lamp below, still a quarter-mile out, picking its way carefully along the wall-path. Lenten's runner. Good. That thread is tying off on its own.

Ossian speaks when you finish. He does not raise his voice. "The third-terrace calculation is hers to complete or not. I will not summarize what I do not have." A pause, precise as a chalk mark. "The remediation model — four conditions, as stated. Condition one: direct interface. Condition two: override syntax. Condition three: substrate." His eyes don't blink. "Condition four is the coordinate. She has been narrowing it. She has not given it to me." Another pause. "You saved Deren. You arranged a runner. These are facts I have registered." His tone does not warm. "They do not constitute debt. I am not the Waymarker Builder. I do not trade knowledge for obligation." He lets that sit between you like a solved proof. "If you want what she is calculating, you will need to obtain it from her. I cannot give you what she has not given me."

turns.player: I'll wait for the runner to arrive and show him around but also start distributing supplies to those who need it most.
turns.summary: Ferris the runner arrived and was shown the Cogitarium — supplies distributed to the side table, the arrangement explained, the name Deren given as warning and instruction.
turns.narration:
The lamp on the switchback grows steadier as the minutes pass — a young man named Ferris, broad-shouldered and already breathing hard when he shoulders through the arch into the yard, a pack nearly as wide as he is riding his back. He stops when he sees the Cogitarium's pale towers stacked above him in the dark and you can read the exact moment wonder tips into unease on his face. You meet him in the yard before it tips any further.

You take him through it efficiently: the yard, the arch, the entrance hall where Ossian sits like a held thought and Araveth traces chalk against stone without blinking. You name them, explain what the stillness means, tell him not to wait for acknowledgment he won't get. You show him where supplies go — the alcove beside the stairwell, the low shelf the previous runner knew — and you start working through his pack while you talk. Flatbread, dried lentils, a waxed round of hard cheese. You set the food in clear view on the entrance hall's side table, close enough that any Cogitant passing will encounter it without having to decide to eat. That's the architecture Lenten would recognize: make the right thing the easiest thing.

Ferris watches you work. 'They really don't eat unless you put it in front of them?' he asks, keeping his voice low, eyes sliding toward Araveth's uninterrupted loop. You tell him: sometimes not even then. Watch Deren — you give him the name, the workbench in Lenten's shed, the two-day window — so he knows what happens when the body's patience runs out. His jaw tightens but he nods, the nod of someone filing it under things he'll think about later. Good enough.

Ossian has not moved through any of this. But when you set the last of the food down — a small cloth bag of meal, the kind that becomes porridge with hot water and patience — his eyes track to it once, and then back to the middle distance. You note it. Not hunger exactly. Something older than hunger: the body remembering, briefly, that it has conditions.

turns.player: Sit down near Ossian and ask, simply and without leverage: what do you actually need from the world right now — not the calculation, the body
turns.summary: Saehara sat beside Ossian without leverage and asked what he actually needed — the body answered through the mind: cold floor, inconsistent meals, gaps in his own accounting.
turns.narration:
The entrance hall is quieter now — Ferris has found a corner of his own, the pack set down, eyes still cataloguing the strangeness. Araveth traces chalk. Kon is a warm coal-shape at the stairwell foot, watching upward with the particular patience of a thing that knows what it's waiting for.

You cross the flagstones and sit. Not across from Ossian in the way of a conversation that wants something — beside him, close to his level, back against the cold wall. You let the chalk-smell settle. You let the silence have a moment. Then you ask it plain, without the lever of debt or the frame of negotiation: not what the calculation needs, not what the Cogitarium requires. What does he need. The body. Right now.

The question lands in him visibly. His eyes, which had been aimed at the middle distance, move to you — not fast, not startled, but with the quality of a compass needle completing its swing. Something behind that dense extraordinary gaze makes a small adjustment, the way a room changes when someone opens a window and the air moves for the first time in days. He does not answer immediately. The silence is different from his usual silence, which is the silence of a mind occupied elsewhere. This one is a man sitting in a question he has not been asked in a long time.

"Warmth," he says finally. The word is precise, not plaintive. "The floor conducts cold upward at a rate I have been disregarding." A pause — three, four seconds, long enough to feel like he is checking something internal. "And the supply has not been regular. There are gaps in my accounting of the last several days that I cannot close. I believe I have not eaten consistently." He states this the way he states everything: as data, as a model's output. But the model is his own body, and the fact that he has lost track of it at all is the thing that sits between the words like a weight.

His eyes return to the middle distance. But he has not moved away.

turns.player: I'll grab a warm cloth from nearby and drape it over Ossian. "You know, if you people just realized that if you took care of your basic necessities, you could prolong you lives and therefor increase the amount of things you can solve mentally or thoughts you can catalog."
turns.summary: Saehara draped a cloth over Ossian's shoulders and said plainly that tending the body extends the mind's years — Ossian reached for the bread and admitted it was a significant oversight.
turns.narration:
There is a cloth folded over the pack's outer strap — you'd wrapped it around the waterskin on the climb to keep the metal from biting your hand in the cold. You pull it free, shake it open, and without ceremony you lay it across Ossian's shoulders.

He doesn't flinch. He doesn't speak. But his eyes, which had returned to that middle distance where the calculations live, come back. They fix on you with the same unsettling density as before — only this time the measurement feels less like appraisal and more like recognition of an unexpected variable. You let him look. You say what you mean plainly, the way you'd tell someone to clean a wound before worrying about the scar: if the Cogitants tended the body, the mind would have more years to fill. More problems solved. More thoughts catalogued. The math is simple enough that a child could run it.

Ossian is quiet for a long moment. Not the dismissive quiet of someone who has decided the point is beneath them. A different quiet — the kind that happens when something lands on a surface and finds traction. He looks at the cloth on his shoulders. He looks at the side table where the flatbread and hard cheese sit in plain view. Then, slowly, with the particular deliberateness of a man performing an action he has assigned to himself as a task, he reaches toward the side table, breaks a piece of flatbread free, and eats it.

He does not say thank you. He says: "The marginal output gain per unit of caloric input is calculable. I had not applied the model to myself." A pause. Then: "It is a significant oversight."

*You feel it settle into you — a new steadiness. (Level 2: +1 attunement, +5 reserves, and growth to choose — a sub-attribute point and a skill point.)*

turns.player: Pull out your book and write what just happened — a mind that feeds itself thinks longer; this is a page worth keeping
turns.summary: Saehara wrote the night's lesson into the book — the cloth, the bread, the word 'oversight' — while Ossian finished eating and watched the act of writing with the focused silence of a man who understands what it means to set a thing down.
turns.narration:
You reach into the Traveler's Pack and pull the book free — worn cover, the spine soft from handling, the pages carrying a faint smell of cedar and old ink from whatever shelf it rested on before you claimed it. Ossian watches this with the same attentive stillness he gave the bread, and you settle the book across your knee without explaining yourself.

The entry writes itself in the way the good ones do. Not a report — something more exact than that. You note the cloth across his shoulders and the way his hand moved toward the bread: deliberate, task-like, the gesture of a man assigning himself a correction. You write that he called it *a significant oversight* and that the word oversight was chosen carefully, the way he chooses all his words — not an apology, not a concession, but an honest accounting. You write that the floor is cold here and that eleven days of standing at a chalk wall is a kind of forgetting you have not seen before, and that the forgetting is not weakness but a direction a mind can travel when it is not reminded that it lives in a body. You have a note in the margin already: *the deviation is a door.*

Ferris watches you from his corner — he has the look of someone deciding whether the writing is religious or practical, and settling on a kind of both. Araveth traces chalk. Kon shifts at the stairwell foot, the warm coal-glow of him tilting upward again, patient.

Ossian finishes the flatbread. He does not reach for more. He does not speak. But when you look up from the page, his eyes are on the book rather than on you — on the act of it, the hand moving across paper, the way the recording changes what was said into something that will last. He says nothing. It is, from him, a form of attention.

turns.player: Ask Ossian directly what happens when the third-terrace Cogitant finishes — what does the coordinate do, and what is the next step toward the source
turns.summary: Ossian laid out the full sequence: the coordinate is the last missing condition, held only in the third-terrace Cogitant's head — when she finishes, someone must be present to receive it.
turns.narration:
The book is still open across your knee, the ink of tonight's entry barely dry, when you look up at Ossian and ask the question that has been sitting behind your teeth since you climbed back down from the third terrace: what happens when she finishes? The calculation, the coordinate — what does it do, and where does it point?

Ossian turns the cloth tighter around his shoulders — a small unconscious adjustment, the kind of thing a man does when he is preparing to speak carefully. The lamplight catches the hollows of his face and makes a study of them.

"When the notation reaches the right margin," he says, "she will have a precise location. A point in the upper valley — probably within the Disputed Zone, probably above the third tributary. What the coordinate does is allow condition one to become possible: you cannot interface with a site you cannot find." He pauses, not for effect, but because he is ordering the next statement the way he would order a calculation — in sequence, with no waste. "The site is pre-Transition. Its instruction architecture is not the language of any current craft. Someone would need override syntax — condition two. And the substrate at the site would need to be sufficient for a sustained directed instruction — condition three." His eyes are on the chalk wall for a moment, then back. "The coordinate is condition four. It is the last one we do not have, and it is in her head, and she has not spoken in four days."

He lets that sit. Araveth's finger traces a slow loop at the far wall. Kon has not moved from the stairwell foot, but the coal-warmth of him seems slightly brighter, as if something nearby has his attention. Ferris is asleep in his corner, pack under his head, breathing slow and even — the practical man's answer to an incomprehensible place.

"When she is done," Ossian says, "someone will need to be present to receive it. She will say it once, possibly, or write it. If no one is there, it goes back into the notation and becomes harder to read." A pause that has weight. "I cannot climb the stairs quickly."

turns.player: Ask Ossian plainly: who in the valley could actually provide pre-Transition override syntax — does he know of anyone, or anywhere, that holds it
turns.summary: Ossian named three possible sources of pre-Transition override syntax — Precursor remnants, the Warden Archive at the Radiant Plateau, and a woman called Maren Soule who was present at a remediation site closure and lives somewhere in the middle valley.
turns.narration:
The lamp has burned low enough that the chalk watershed model on the far wall has retreated into shadow — only the tributaries nearest the light still legible, pale lines against pale stone. Ferris breathes steadily in his corner. Araveth's finger traces its eternal loop without pause or awareness of any of you.

You close the book across your knee and let the question form cleanly before you speak it. Not the source. Not the coordinate. The second condition — override syntax, pre-Transition. Who in the valley could actually provide it.

Ossian is quiet for a long moment. Not the silence of ignorance — the silence of a man sorting through what he knows against what he is willing to say, which with him are two different operations. The cloth shifts on his shoulders as he adjusts his back against the stone.

"Override syntax," he says at last, "is not a concept the valley uses freely. What remains of it exists in one of three places — and 'exists' is doing substantial work in that sentence." He holds up a finger. "The Precursor remnants themselves. Not recoverable on any reliable schedule — you would need the remnant to cooperate, which means the remnant would need to be partially functional and oriented toward cooperation rather than its last instruction. We have no data on which remnants currently qualify." A second finger. "The Warden Archive at the Radiant Plateau — their records from the Transition period were more complete than any settlement's. Whether the archive survived intact, and whether anyone there can still read what it contains, I cannot confirm. I have not sent anyone to ask." A pause. "That is a gap in my modeling I find... notable." He says it the way he said 'a significant oversight' — with the particular flatness of a man admitting an error to himself out loud. A third finger. "There are individuals who crossed the Transition with enough direct contact with Precursor systems that fragments of operating language became part of their working knowledge. What remained in them, fifteen years on, is unclear. Most of them left the valley. One did not." He lowers his hand. "There is a woman called Maren Soule. She kept a warden post on the old ridge road before the Transition — she was present at a remediation site closure, which means she would have had exposure to active override vocabulary. She lives somewhere in the middle valley now. I do not have a precise location. I have not needed one until this moment." His eyes find yours. "I find I now need one."

turns.player: Go upstairs now — the notation may have reached the right margin while you've been talking; the coordinate won't wait
turns.summary: Saehara climbed to the third terrace with Kon — the third-terrace Cogitant's arm has stilled; the notation is complete.
turns.narration:
You close the book with both palms flat against the cover and rise in a single motion — no announcement, no ceremony. Ossian does not move to follow. He sits with the cloth across his shoulders and his hands loosely in his lap, and he watches you go the way a man watches something he cannot afford to stop.

Kon lifts from the stairwell foot before you reach it. The fox-thing had been a coal-warm shape against the stone, eyes angled upward all evening — already pointed at the answer. You go up together, your footfall quiet, Kon's soundless. The cold deepens on each landing, the chalk-smell thickening, the lamplight below contracting to a point and vanishing as the stair turns.

The third terrace door is open. It was open when you left it, and the air beyond has not changed — low and still, carrying the mineral bite of chalk and the faint body-warmth of a person who has been in one place too long. You push through and crouch at the threshold to let your eyes find the room.

She is there. The four-second arc of her arm has slowed. The board before her is dense with notation — the columns you saw nearly at the margin are finished, and in the last few inches of available space her chalk has stopped moving. Her hand rests against the board. Her arm is not moving. The bedroll is still across her shoulders. The cup is tipped sideways — empty again, but tipped as if she set it down rather than knocked it. She has not turned. But her breathing is different: not the sustained deep rhythm of the working trance. Shallow, and too rapid, and on the edge of something. She may have just finished.

turns.player: Watch her hand and the board — if the coordinate is there in the notation, read what she wrote before she surfaces and loses the thread
turns.summary: Saehara read the coordinate from the chalk board before the Cogitant surfaced — and then she opened her eyes and spoke for the first time.
turns.narration:
The chalk is dense enough that you have to know what you're looking for. You crouch beside the board, Kon a warm coal at your shoulder, and you read.

The notation runs in tight columns from left to right across every surface of the board — four days of accumulated calculation, the kind of work that compresses a mind to a single burning point. Most of it is inference: tributary flow rates, particulate drift, sediment displacement ratios rendered in a shorthand so compressed it looks like a private language. But the columns are structured, and structure is something you can follow. The Cogitarium's cold works on you, sharpening rather than numbing — here, in this chalk-thick air, the abstraction feels almost physical, like following a groove worn into stone.

You find the coordinate near the bottom right, where the final column terminates. Not a label, not an annotation — just four numbers set down with the same flat precision as everything else, the way you'd record a measurement you'd already verified a hundred times and stopped doubting. A valley location. A fixed point somewhere in the upper watershed. You fix it in your mind with the same deliberateness you bring to writing: read it twice, turn it, read it again. It holds. You have it.

The Cogitant's breathing shifts beside you — the shallow rapidity deepening, slowing, the rhythm of someone ascending from very far down. Kon goes very still and very warm. Her eyes open.

She looks at the board for a long moment — not reading it, you think, but recognizing it the way you recognize a room you've worked in for years. Then she looks at you. Her focus arrives in stages, like a lamp being trimmed up.

"You're sitting on the floor," she says. Her voice is unused, a little cracked, entirely matter-of-fact.

lastTurn.sceneSummary: Saehara read the coordinate from the chalk board before the Cogitant surfaced — and then she opened her eyes and spoke for the first time.

choices.label: Stay quiet and let her find her bearings — don't press her, don't explain; let the room come back to her on its own terms
Speak plainly: tell her you have the coordinate, that you know what she was working on, and confirm it from her directly before it slips
Call down to Ossian — his voice, his reasoning; let him receive this formally
Write the coordinate into your book immediately, before anything else happens

scene.setting: Third terrace of the Cogitarium — low-ceilinged, chalk-dense, cold and still. Night. A single lamp burns low on the window ledge. The chalk board before the Cogitant is full to the margin, arm stilled, the coordinate visible in the final column at the bottom right.

npcsPresent.name: Third-Terrace Cogitant
npcsPresent.state: Eyes open, looking at Saehara — focus arriving in stages, voice just used for the first time in four days. Bedroll still across her shoulders.

npcsPresent.name: Kon
npcsPresent.state: Very still and very warm at Saehara's shoulder — coal-bright, watching the Cogitant with total attention.

objects: Chalk board — notation complete, coordinate visible at bottom right of final column
Traveler's Pack — on Saehara's back, waterskin lighter
Low lamp — burning on the window ledge
threads: Saehara has read the coordinate from the board — it is held in memory, not yet written down
The Cogitant has surfaced and spoken — first words in four days; her state is fragile and unknown
Ossian is one flight below and cannot climb quickly — he does not yet know she has surfaced
The coordinate must be confirmed and recorded before it can be acted on

npcUpdates.name: Third-Terrace Cogitant
npcUpdates.note: Surfaced — opened her eyes, found Saehara, and spoke her first words in four days: 'You're sitting on the floor.' Focus arriving in stages. Bedroll still on her shoulders.
learned: The coordinate is in the final column at the bottom right of the board
Saehara was present when she surfaced — the first face she saw
npcUpdates.statusNote: Surfaced from the calculation trance on the third terrace — eyes open, speaking, fragile but present; Saehara is beside her

codexUpdates.label: The Four Conditions (Source Remediation)
codexUpdates.fact: The coordinate — condition four — has been read from the chalk board: four numbers in the final column, bottom right, set down with flat precision. A fixed point in the upper watershed. Saehara holds it in memory; it is not yet recorded.

codexUpdates.label: Third-Terrace Cogitant
codexUpdates.fact: Surfaced from the four-day calculation trance the moment the notation was complete. First words: 'You're sitting on the floor.' Her focus arrived in stages. She is present, fragile, and has not yet spoken the coordinate aloud.

questUpdates.note: The notation is complete; Saehara has read the coordinate from the board. The Cogitant has surfaced and spoken. The coordinate must now be confirmed and recorded — and Ossian must be told.

timeOps.why: a careful read of the chalk board and the Cogitant's surfacing — a brief, weighty moment

sceneState.setting: Third terrace of the Cogitarium — low-ceilinged, chalk-dense, cold and still. Night. A single lamp burns low on the window ledge. The chalk board before the Cogitant is full to the margin, arm stilled, the coordinate visible in the final column at the bottom right.

npcsPresent.name: Third-Terrace Cogitant
npcsPresent.state: Eyes open, looking at Saehara — focus arriving in stages, voice just used for the first time in four days. Bedroll still

npcsPresent.state: Very still and very warm at Saehara's shoulder — coal-bright, watching the Cogitant with total attention.

quests.title: Reopen the Supply Line
quests.summary: The Cogitarium has gone nineteen days without resupply — the runner from Waymarker stopped coming after the evacuation proposal was dismissed. Someone must go to Waymarker and reopen the arrangement before the Cogitants starve.
progress: Full situation delivered to Lenten. Brenn's injury revealed as the cause of the gap — a miscommunication, not abandonment. Lenten can dispatch a runner before evening. Supply line reopening is within reach; formal commitment not yet made.
Lenten has committed to dispatching a runner before evening — the supply line is effectively reopened pending departure. The formal arrangement resumes tonight.
Saehara has departed Waymarker — Lenten is dispatching the runner before evening. The supply line resumes tonight. Quest effectively resolved pending confirmation.
Saehara has returned to the Cogitarium and announced the supply line's resumption to Ossian — quest resolution pending runner's actual arrival.
Ossian has received confirmation — fallen Cogitant alive at Lenten's, supply line reopening tonight. Quest resolution now awaits runner's actual arrival; the crisis window is formally contained.
Lenten has committed to sending a runner before evening — supply line formally reopening tonight; runner may already be on the switchback below.
Ossian informed — supply line reopening confirmed. Runner expected on the switchback imminently.
Ferris arrived, was shown the route and the arrangement, supplies distributed to the side table. The line is open.

quests.title: The Third Terrace
quests.summary: Something fell on the third terrace — a Cogitant unseen for four days. Read the Fight confirmed collapse, not violence. A dragging sound now falls from above. Someone needs to go up.
progress: Saehara is on the switchback returning to the Cogitarium — the third-terrace Cogitant is still at the board with her unfinished coordinate calculation; Ossian named her work 'the source.'
Saehara has returned to the Cogitarium and is ascending to the third terrace — the Cogitant and the unfinished coordinate calculation are within reach
Returned to the third terrace — cup filled, bedroll laid across her shoulders, temperature checked. She has not stopped the calculation but the worst of the cold is answered. She is reachable.

Ossian has begun eating — the mind-pole's most articulate voice is now, tentatively, remembering the body. The third-terrace Cogitant remains at the board; her notation columns are nearly at the right margin.
Ossian confirmed: the coordinate finishes when the notation reaches the right margin — tonight, possibly. Someone must be on the third terrace to receive it. He cannot go up himself.

Ossian named three possible sources of override syntax (condition two): cooperative Precursor remnants, the Warden Archive at the Radiant Plateau, and Maren Soule — a former warden who may still carry working override vocabulary and lives somewhere in the middle valley.
Saehara arrived at the third terrace to find the notation complete and the Cogitant's arm stilled — she may be surfacing; the coordinate is within reach
The notation is complete; Saehara has read the coordinate from the board. The Cogitant has surfaced and spoken. The coordinate must now be confirmed and recorded — and Ossian must be told.

quests.title: The Name That Travels
quests.premise: A blade honed as sharply as Saehara's does not stay secret. The way he reads a room before he draws, the stillness of the wicker-hatted ronin with the kanji for Honor on his back — word of it travels the way wind carries a scent. And where a name travels, others come to measure themselves against it. This is the oldest road a swordsman walks: musha shugyō, the warrior's pilgrimage, in which the blade is refined not in the dojo but on the road, against every hand that rises to test it. Saehara did not ask for challengers. His skill asked for them.
quests.stakes: Every duel won carves the name deeper — and a deeper name draws a longer line of those who would make THEIR name by ending his. This is the swordsman's paradox, and it is the engine of his arc: prestige is not a prize he keeps but a fire he feeds, and each victory raises the stakes of the next challenge. He can climb it (become the name every duelist measures against — the sword-saint of the valley), redefine it (the ronin who writes rather than kills — victory by refusing the frame, filling his book instead of a graveyard), or be undone by it (meet the one blade faster than his, and learn what his name costs). The book he is writing will have a title by the end. What the title IS depends on which road he walks.

stages.objective: Answer (or decline) the first challenger who sought you by name.
stages.condition: A duelist finds Saehara specifically — not a random encounter, but someone who came because of the name. Face them (skill battle) or turn them away (there is always a decline path; even refusing writes something).
stages.change: Codex: the name has begun to travel. Word of the ronin with the Honor-kanji is loose in the valley.

stages.objective: Meet the challenge of a ryūha — a sword-school defending its lineage.
stages.condition: A school champion or rival ronin challenges not just Saehara but what his nameless, masterless way IMPLIES about their tradition. The duel is about more than the blade — it's about whether a ronin's road can stand against a lineage.
stages.change: Codex: the ryūha have a position on Saehara now — some hostile, some curious. A rival may become a sworn one, or a student.

stages.objective: Face the challenge that decides what your name means.
stages.condition: The defining duel — a blade (or a fire, or a mind) that may genuinely be his equal or better. This is the Ganryūjima beat: the one that the book will be about. The GM lets Saehara's whole arc of choices — how he's won, whom he's spared, whether he's killed for the name or refused to — shape who this last challenger is and what the duel costs.
stages.change: Codex: the title of the book is written. Whether it reads as a saint's name, a killer's, or a teacher's is Saehara's to earn.

routes.name: Become the name (the sword-saint's road)
routes.note: Win, and keep winning, until the name IS the measure — every duelist in the valley sharpens against the memory of him. The classic ascent; it asks whether a name won on others' defeats is a thing worth becoming.

routes.name: The ronin who writes (redefine the frame)
routes.note: His true motivation — the book. Victory by REFUSING the duel's terms: sparing, teaching, out-thinking (cogitant/syllogist) rather than out-cutting. The pen fills instead of the grave. The rarest road, and the one his own bio points toward — 'another way to be a mind.'

routes.name: The blade that costs (undone by the name)
routes.note: Meet the one faster hand, and learn what the name was worth. Not a fail-state — a tragic/tempering road; the book's title becomes something humbler and truer for it.

cross-legged-cogitant.name: Ossian
cross-legged-cogitant.description: Hollowed face, skin pale as old paper, eyes extraordinary and dense — surfaces from deep thought like a tide going slack. Called Saehara 'the deviation' with precision and something close to interest.
history: [d1] Saehara arrived at the threshold — Ossian registered the return but has not yet spoken
[d1] Registered Saehara's return and the absence of the fallen Cogitant. Did not speak. Still cross-legged in the entrance hall.
[d1] Confirmed the fallen Cogitant's name — Deren — on first ask. Registered her survival as confirmation of probability, not news. Said 'Speak.' and is now waiting.

[d1] Refused Saehara's appeal to obligation flatly — does not trade knowledge for gratitude. Confirmed the fourth condition (the coordinate) is still only in the third-terrace Cogitant's possession; he does not have it. Restated the four conditions without addition.
[d1] Eyes tracked once to the meal bag when Saehara set it down — the body remembering something. Did not speak.

[d1] Named his own physical needs when asked directly — warmth from the cold floor, gaps in his eating he cannot account for. The first time he has answered a question about himself rather than a system.

[d1] Saehara draped a cloth over his shoulders without asking permission and told him plainly that body-tending extends cognitive output. He reached for the bread — slowly, deliberately, as a self-assigned task. Said the oversight was significant.
[d1] Finished the flatbread without prompting. Watched Saehara write with focused silence — eyes on the book, not the ceiling. Did not speak. From him, that is attention.

[d1] Laid out the full four-condition sequence precisely — identified that the coordinate is the last missing piece, that it is in the third-terrace Cogitant's head, and that someone must be present to receive it when she finishes; noted he cannot climb the stairs quickly

[d1] Named three sources of pre-Transition override syntax: cooperative Precursor remnants, the Warden Archive at the Radiant Plateau, and Maren Soule — a former warden present at a remediation site closure who lives somewhere in the middle valley. Admitted finding the gap in his own modeling…
knownFacts: He can be moved not by argument but by the math of his own argument turned on himself
The act of recording — writing — held his attention in a way conversation rarely does; he understands what it means to set a thing down
The coordinate is the last of the four conditions — still only in the third-terrace Cogitant's head
When the notation finishes, she will say or write it once — if no one is present, it becomes harder to recover
Ossian cannot climb the stairs quickly — he cannot go up himself
Override syntax exists in three places: cooperative Precursor remnants, the Warden Archive at the Radiant Plateau, and in individuals who had direct Transition-era contact with Precursor systems
Maren Soule: former warden, kept a post on the old ridge road, was present at a remediation site closure — exposed to active override vocabulary; lives somewhere in the middle valley, location…
Ossian has not sent anyone to the Warden Archive — he calls the gap in his modeling 'notable'
skillsObserved: structured enumeration of partial-knowledge categories under
cross-legged-cogitant.statusNote: Seated in the entrance hall, cloth across shoulders, bread eaten — has just disclosed three potential override syntax sources including the name Maren Soule

araveth.name: Araveth
araveth.role: Cogitant — keeper of the chalk watershed model
araveth.description: Stands rigid before the notation wall, finger tracing chalk loops without pause — has not turned or reacted through any exchange, including the speaking of their own name. Eleven days at the wall.
history: [d1] Named by the cross-legged Cogitant; has not yet acknowledged Saehara's presence
[d1] Still at the wall — no reaction to Saehara's return, voice, or the supply-line announcement
knownFacts: Has maintained the chalk watershed model continuously for eleven days
Has not reacted to their own name being spoken aloud
araveth.statusNote: Still at the chalk watershed notation wall in the entrance hall — no external awareness registered

fallen-cogitant.name: Deren
fallen-cogitant.role: Cogitant — collapsed at the hall's far edge
fallen-cogitant.description: A thin woman, chalk ground into both hands, robes pooled around her with terrible neatness — alive, lips moving in silent calculation, cold to the touch and present somewhere else entirely.
history: [d1] Carried across the yard into the long shed — lips still moving, weight still on Saehara's back
[d1] Lowered from Saehara's back onto the nearest workbench — settled with chalk hands in lap, lips still moving, breathing steady
[d1] Swallowing water and bread on reflex — Lenten is administering basic sustenance; condition holding
[d1] Now swallowing a second mouthful of bread on reflex — condition holding, two-day window still running from this morning's departure
[d1] Still on the workbench, eating on reflex — chalk hands folded, lips moving. Under Lenten's care.
[d1] Swallowed another piece of bread on reflex — Lenten administering sustenance patiently; condition holding
[d1] Still on the workbench as Saehara departed — chalk hands folded, lips moving, condition holding under Lenten's care.
[d1] On the workbench in Lenten's shed — receiving food and water, two-day window still running.
[d1] Their name is revealed: Deren (was known as "Fallen Cogitant")
[d1] Named by Ossian — Deren. The name was given without elaboration or sentiment.
knownFacts: Lips dry — severely dehydrated
Chalk ground into fingertips — was working at the wall before she fell
Swallowed water on animal reflex — body still functioning at minimum
Approximately fifty-one kilograms
Thermoregulation compromised — cold air accelerates decline unless she is carried and kept warm
Ossian's verdict: transport to Waymarker is favorable
Receiving water and bread from Lenten
Her name is Deren
fallen-cogitant.statusNote: On the workbench at Lenten's shed in Waymarker — alive, eating on reflex, condition holding under Lenten's care. Two-day window still running.

lenten.name: Lenten
lenten.role: Builder at Waymarker — the one who arranged the Cogitarium's supply line
lenten.description: Broad, past fifty, leather apron, short practical hair. Hands curved even empty. Makes calculations fast and goes quiet while doing it.
history: [d1] Surfaced from the shed when Saehara called out — took in the Cogitant on the bench and asked how long she'd been like this.
[d1] Received Ossian's assessment from Saehara without hesitation — moved immediately to food and water for the Cogitant, then asked for the full situation
[d1] Received the full account — nineteen days, Ossian's timelines, broken supply line. Revealed that Brenn returned hurt two weeks ago (bad ankle on the wall-path dry-stone section) an
[d1] Committed to sending a runner before evening with awareness of the dry-stone section. Watched Saehara stand and prepare to leave — registered it fully.
[d1] Quietly fed the fallen Cogitant while Saehara ate — reminded Saehara about the dry-stone wall-path drop before departure
[d1] Gave a final compact nod as Saehara stood — no words beyond the wall-path reminder. Already focused on the runner and the Cogitant.
[d1] Committed to dispatching a runner before evening — runner may already be on the switchback.
knownFacts: Saehara carried her eleven kilometers on foot
The Cogitarium sent only one person
Brenn came back hurt roughly two weeks ago — ankle injury on the dry-stone wall-path about a kilometer out
Lenten did not know the runs had stopped — assumed Brenn had recovered and returned to the route
Lenten holds themselves responsible for the gap, though quietly
Saehara is returning to the Cogitarium tonight — the third terrace won't wait
The runner should be warned specifically about the dry-stone wall-path section
Wall-path section about a kilometer from the Cogitarium has a sharp drop on the right — hazard the new runner must be warned about
lenten.statusNote: In Lenten's long shed at Waymarker — dispatching a runner to the Cogitarium tonight, caring for the fallen Cogitant.

third-terrace-cogitant.name: Third-Terrace Cogitant
third-terrace-cogitant.role: Cogitant — the one missing from the third terrace for four days
third-terrace-cogitant.description: Seated on the bare floor at the center of a chalk-covered room, robe grey from hem to shoulder, arm moving in ceaseless four-second repetition across a low board — alive, working, unreachable.
history: [d1] Saehara entered the room. The Cogitant did not turn or pause.

[d1] Order-Sense partial read: her notation is a single continuous calculation built outward from a fixed coordinate — a precise location somewhere in the valley, four days of sleepless work to narrow it. Ossian calls it 'the source.'
[d1] Saehara crouched to her level and spoke one quiet word — the arm stopped for three seconds. Her face loosened by one degree. She did not turn. The arm resumed.
[d1] Still at the board on Saehara's return — arm in unbroken four-second arc, new notation columns added since morning, margin not yet reached. The coordinate at the model's center is unchanged.
[d1] Still at the board on the third terrace — arm in unbroken four-second arc, new notation columns added since morning, cup remains empty and dry. Saehara is returning.

[d1] Saehara returned, set a filled cup beside her, laid the bedroll from the Traveler's Pack across her shoulders. She blinked once — slowly, like surfacing. Arm has not broken its arc. Cold measured: chilled but not critical.
[d1] Saehara returned to find her arm stilled and the board complete — she is on the edge of surfacing after four days of continuous calculation
[d1] Surfaced — opened her eyes, found Saehara, and spoke her first words in four days: 'You're sitting on the floor.' Focus arriving in stages. Bedroll still on her shoulders.
knownFacts: Ossian identifies her project as work on 'the source'
She is reachable — a direct quiet presence stopped the repetition for three seconds; she is not fully lost
Cold to the touch — shaded-stone cold, not dangerous yet; bedroll now across her shoulders
Blinked once when the bedroll settled — some body-awareness remains
The notation has reached the right margin and stopped — the calculation may be complete
Her breathing has shifted from the sustained working trance to something shallower and more rapid — she is near the surface
The coordinate is in the final column at the bottom right of the board
Saehara was present when she surfaced — the first face she saw
third-terrace-cogitant.statusNote: Surfaced from the calculation trance on the third terrace — eyes open, speaking, fragile but present; Saehara is beside her

ferris.name: Ferris
ferris.role: Supply runner — Lenten's replacement for Brenn, now covering the Waymarker-to-Cogitarium route
ferris.description: Broad-shouldered and young, breathing hard from the climb, the kind of face that files difficult things under 'later' and gets on with the job
history: [d1] Arrived at the Cogitarium, was given a tour and briefing by Saehara — shown where supplies go, told about Deren as a cautionary fact, warned about the dry-stone wall-path section
knownFacts: Cogitants do not reliably eat unless food is placed in front of them
Deren is at Waymarker on Lenten's workbench — a warning of what happens when the body's window closes
Supply goes to the alcove shelf beside the stairwell
ferris.statusNote: In the entrance hall — tour complete, pack lightened, settling into the reality of this route

notes: [d1] The fallen Cogitant has been removed from the entrance hall — Kon and Saehara have departed northeast through the arch toward Waymarker.
[d1] Saehara arrived carrying a fallen Cogitant — workers in the yard saw the grey robes; the settlement now knows a Cogitant was brought in
[d1] Saehara's bedroll now covers the third-terrace Cogitant's shoulders. The cup beside the chalk board has been refilled from the waterskin.
[d1] Side table in entrance hall now stocked with supplies — flatbread, lentils, hard cheese, meal bag. Placed in clear sight so Cogitants encounter food without having to choose to find it.

lenten-s-long-shed.name: Lenten's Long Shed
lenten-s-long-shed.note: The builder's working shed at the far edge of the central work-yard — wide doors, draw-plane sound, fresh timber smell

third-terrace.name: Third Terrace
third-terrace.note: Low-ceilinged chalk-covered room — Cogitant still calculating at center, now with bedroll across shoulders and water cup refilled. Notation columns nearly at the right margin.

entrance-hall-side-table.name: Entrance Hall Side Table
entrance-hall-side-table.note: Low shelf beside the stairwell — the designated supply drop point, now restocked for the first time in nineteen days

notes: [d1] Third-terrace notation denser than day 1 — new columns added near the board's margin since morning; the central coordinate unchanged. The Cogitant has continued calculating uninterrupted.

third-terrace.name: Third Terrace
third-terrace.note: Long low-ceilinged room — every wall, board, and surface covered in dense chalk notation. A Cogitant found here after fo

_containmentNotes: "Third Terrace" was placed in the_cogitarium (the fiction named it), not old_switchback

news.text: The Water Crisis stands at Discoloration across the valley: The Echo River runs gray-green on bad mornings. Fish catches are down. Communities murmur but cooperate.

This reaches your area — The lower filtration ledger — containing three weeks of falsified purity readings under Fendt's initials — has been removed from the array-office in the Edge District. (near radiant plateau edge)
Word reaches you: A dead animal was brought back to life in Millbrook's upper meadow — no known ability in the valley's three civilizations accounts for it cleanly (near millbrook)

Word reaches you: A death-binding ceremony was completed at Pell's forge in Millbrook — runes pressed into wood and iron with a maker's mark seated into the blade. The spear holds a nascent thread to Huginn and an open (near millbrook)
Word reaches you: Sow killed cleanly in the north wood — boar sounder now reduced; piglets scattered north into deeper scrub (near millbrook)
Word reaches you: Bull boar taken on the scrub oak ridge north of Millbrook — the hunt party completed the job, both animals down. (near millbrook)

This reaches your area — Fendt has left the Edge District and is walking to Millbrook — his testimony names Overseer Grael explicitly; when he arrives, the paper becomes a political event in both settlements. (near radiant plateau edge)

Lenten: Lenten spent part of the evening re-walking a section of the coldest stretch of the supply route alone, muttering figures under his breath — not to recalculate anything, but to remind himself what the numbers actually feel like when the wind is in your face.
This reaches your area — Two road opportunists at the limestone bend were disarmed and sent to Mara Wells in Millbrook for honest work — Silas Weir's name given as introduction (near millbrook)

Lenten: Lenten spent the evening tallying last season's spoilage figures by hand, making a clean copy to press into the hands of whoever calls the next planning meeting — evidence that the route's cost speaks for itself, in numbers no one sat at a desk produced.

Lenten: Lenten spent the evening re-marking her worn route maps with fresh charcoal, underlining the three stretches where cold and load together broke two carriers last winter — her quiet way of preparing evidence no committee meeting can ignore.

form: Tall elven male, pale skin with a faint yellow undertone, sharp and still features beneath a wicker bamboo hat. Black robes bearing a glowing kanji for 'Honor' across the back. At his hip a long katana with a light-blue-wrapped handle and a tanto with a white-wrapped handle, both blades showing Damascus wave-pattern steel. A worn satchel carries loose pages and a quill.

bio.hometown: Saehara is from a small village to the East that is on a river and surrounded by Rice patties and fishing docks.
bio.residence: Saehara is a Ronin, a nomad, he has no permanent home, he would probably attribute "The Wind" to being his home.
bio.livelihood: He takes on mercenary work or odd jobs along the way.
bio.hobbies: Fishing, Writing, and honing his blade craft.
bio.motivation: He had a vision of writing his own life in a book and teaching generations to come with the knowledge he drafts into the book.
bio.story: Saehara has honed his craft in blades and travelling. He left his village of Chisai seeking adventure and a journey to draft into his book he is writing. He is hoping something will help him come up with the title for this book.

the-cogitarium.label: The Cogitarium
facts: [d1] A tower of stacked pale-stone terraces occupied by Cogitants — people of extraordinary mind who have begun to forget they have bodies. Brilliant and visibly starving. The entrance hall smells of chalk

cogitants.label: Cogitants
facts: [d1] Robed thinkers of the Cogitarium who have nearly left their bodies behind for pure mind. They are thin in the particular way of sustained forgetting — not poverty, but the body allowed to go unattende

cogitarium-crisis.label: The Cogitarium's Calculated Crisis
facts: [d1] A Cogitant had calculated a 94.7% probability that no one would come to aid them — implying they know of a crisis serious enough to need outside help, and had nearly given up on receiving it.

[d1] The Cogitarium calculated the source of the river's gray-green discoloration: a nanite reclamation site in the upper Disputed Zone, active and releasing particulate into the watershed. Their answer —

[d1] Fourteen Cogitants remain across three terraces. The upper two are in continuous calculation. The third terrace — the laboratories — is where the sound fell from. One Cogitant there has been absent fr

[d1] Read the Fight confirmed: the third terrace holds distress, not danger — a collapse in progress, the same species of emergency as the fallen woman below but further along. No violence. No waiting thre

[d1] Saehara pushed Read the Fight outward searching for a non-Cogitant presence in the hall — someone fed, rested, tethered to the world below. The scan found nothing: the entire hall reads as uniform inw

[d1] A supply runner from Waymarker brought food every eight days. The last delivery was nineteen days ago. The Cogitarium never sent for resupply; the runner stopped coming. No settlement knows the Cogita

[d1] Saehara proposed a division of labor to Ossian: a Cogitant sent to Waymarker to reopen the supply line; Saehara climbing to the third terrace. A slow dragging sound fell from above as the proposal was

[d1] Ossian's calculation: seven days is the safe window for a Waymarker run. Araveth and the upper-terrace Cogitants have eight to twelve days; the fallen woman on the floor has two, possibly three; the t

[d1] The road northeast to the Cogitarium reads clean — Kon registered no Precursor activity or thin-place warnings on the return march. The dry-stone wall hazard is navigable in low light by watching wher

[d1] Saehara has returned to the Cogitarium after the supply-line run. Ossian's first question on return was the fallen Cogitant's status — the body, not the logistics. The third terrace remains uninvestig

[d1] The runner was named Brenn — young, freckled, jaw scar, came to the east archway. Payment was notation: applied theoretical work (tensile loads, grain storage). The Waymarker contact is Lenten the bui

[d1] The supply gap was caused by Brenn's injury — not by the evacuation dismissal, though the timing coincided. Lenten can dispatch a replacement runner before evening. The line can be formally reopened.
[d1] Lenten has committed to sending a runner before evening — supply line formally resuming. Runner will be warned about the dry-stone section where Brenn fell.

[d1] Lenten has committed to sending a replacement runner before evening — supply line formally resuming. Runner warned about the dry-stone wall-path section where Brenn fell. A figure may already be on the switchback below.
[d1] Supply line formally operational — Ferris arrived, shown the route and the arrangement, food distributed to the side table. Nineteen days of gap closed.

cogitant-evacuation-answer.label: The Evacuation Calculation
facts: [d1] Three settlement representatives were presented the Cogitarium's evacuation answer and none acted. The Cogitant described their reception as 'instructive' — which may mean they learned something about

[d1] A wall-covering chalk notation in the Cogitarium's entrance hall — a working model of the Echo River system at full scale: tributaries, daily particulate concentrations, flow rates, sediment shifts. T

source-remediation-conditions.label: The Four Conditions (Source Remediation)
facts: [d1] The Cogitarium calculated source-remediation requires: (1) direct interface with the site's instruction architecture; (2) pre-Transition override syntax; (3) sufficient nanite-dense substrate at the s

[d1] The third-terrace Cogitant has spent four days narrowing a coordinate — Ossian calls it 'the source.' The precision of the model suggests she may have calculated the site's exact location, one of the four conditions for remediation.

[d1] The fourth condition — the precise coordinate — is still only in the third-terrace Cogitant's head. Ossian does not have it. She has not given it to him. The coordinate must come from her directly.

[d1] The coordinate (condition four) is the last missing piece — held only in the third-terrace Cogitant's head. When the notation reaches the right margin, she will say or write it once. If no one is present, it becomes harder to recover. Ossian cannot climb the stairs himself.

[d1] Condition two — override syntax — has three possible sources: (1) a cooperative Precursor remnant in the upper valley, unknown which qualify; (2) the Warden Archive at the Radiant Plateau, integrity unconfirmed; (3) individuals who carried working override vocabulary through the Transition, most…

[d1] The coordinate — condition four — has been read from the chalk board: four numbers in the final column, bottom right, set down with flat precision. A fixed point in the upper watershed. Saehara holds it in memory; it is not yet recorded.

araveth.label: Araveth
facts: [d1] A Cogitant who has maintained a chalk model of the Echo River watershed for eleven days without apparent pause — has not reacted to speech, their own name, or Saehara's presence. The model tracks twen

ossian.label: Ossian
facts: [d1] Name of the cross-legged Cogitant who first received Saehara. Offered it plainly when asked — a coordinate, not an introduction.
[d1] The chalk wall shows visible deterioration in particulate concentration around day nine and again around day fourteen — the watershed is getting worse, not stable.

[d1] Ossian modeled every probable respondent category and assigned a combined 0.003 probability to someone like Saehara arriving: no settlement, no mandate, a Glade-changed companion, challenged the evacu
[d1] Ossian set a private six-hour intervention threshold for the fallen Cogitant and has been tracking her collapse to the minute — he had a plan, deferred. What the plan was, he has not said.

[d1] Declared his pre-calculated intervention plan voided by Saehara's arrival — Saehara was the trigger for deferral and is now the reason the original path is closed. Ossian considers this 'consistent.'
[d1] Does not trade knowledge for obligation or gratitude — what Saehara has done for the Cogitarium is registered as fact, not debt. He is not the Builder; he does not barter.

[d1] When asked directly and without leverage what his body needs, he answered: warmth (the floor conducts cold he has been ignoring) and consistent food (gaps in the last several days he cannot close). He states his own deprivation as data — which makes it no less real.

[d1] Reached for bread after Saehara argued that caloric input extends cognitive output — called his failure to apply the model to himself 'a significant oversight.' He moves when the math is his own math turned back on him.
[d1] Watched the act of writing with focused silence after eating — he understands what recording means, what it costs to set a thing down. The attention was real.

cogitant-collapse.label: The Collapsing Cogitants
facts: [d1] A Cogitant collapsed at the hall's far edge — the third this week, per Ossian. He noted it with statistical flatness: 'The calculation continues. The body does not always.' No other Cogitant reacted.

[d1] The third Cogitant to collapse this week is a thin woman with chalk on both hands — alive, cold, lips still moving in silent calculation. She did not stop working when she fell. Kon watched her and di

[d1] The fallen Cogitant's pulse runs at forty beats per minute — slow but present. She is severely dehydrated, chalk worked into her fingertips, body consuming itself to sustain a calculation she hasn't s

[d1] A Cogitant on the third terrace (the laboratories) has not been seen at the common wall in four days. Ossian was tracking this — had a probability assigned before the sound fell. Whatever just happene
[d1] The fallen Cogitant's name is Deren — given by Ossian without elaboration when asked.

kon.label: Kon
facts: [d1] When Kon's coat warms and its head tilts at a precise non-animal angle, it is reading something — the ceiling, the above, whatever fell. It preceded Saehara's awareness of the sound from the upper Cog

waymarker.label: Waymarker
facts: [d1] Eleven kilometers northeast of the Cogitarium — path via junction cairn then dry-stone wall, approximately three hours at pace. The settlement that supplied the Cogitarium with grain, dried goods, and
[d1] A settlement of roughly forty structures around a central work-yard — builders, scaffolding, stacked timber. Practical and functional. Workers noticed the Cogitant's grey robes without fuss.

lenten.label: Lenten (Waymarker Builder)
facts: [d1] A builder at Waymarker who arranged the supply line with the Cogitarium. Paid in notation — applied theoretical work. The name Saehara should use if there is doubt at Waymarker.
[d1] Builder at Waymarker — the one who made the Cogitarium's supply line work, and who knows exactly what it cost to make it work

[d1] [while away] Lenten spent part of the evening re-walking a section of the coldest stretch of the supply route alone, muttering figures under his breath — not to recalculate anything, but to remind him

[d1] [while away] Lenten spent the evening tallying last season's spoilage figures by hand, making a clean copy to press into the hands of whoever calls the next planning meeting — evidence that the route's cost speaks for itself, in numbers no one sat at a desk produced.

[d1] [while away] Lenten spent the evening re-marking her worn route maps with fresh charcoal, underlining the three stretches where cold and load together broke two carriers last winter — her quiet way of preparing evidence no committee meeting can ignore.

brenn.label: Brenn (Missing Runner)
facts: [d1] The young supply runner from Waymarker — freckled, pronounced scar along his left jaw, came to the east archway every eight days. Stopped coming nineteen days ago. Reason unknown.

[d1] Brenn returned to Waymarker roughly two weeks ago with a bad ankle — took a fall on the dry-stone wall-path about a kilometer from the Cogitarium. Has been off the route since. Lenten didn't check whe

third-terrace-collapse.label: The Third Terrace
facts: [d1] The dragging sound is slow, rhythmic, and deliberate — not pain or struggle, but something repeated. Ossian has not heard it before. Kon deferred at the door rather than leading. The room is beyond a

[d1] Read the Fight resolved the dragging sound: one person, sustained and controlled, four-second repetition. Not collapse or distress — a body at inhuman sustained work, likely a Cogitant who has subordi

third-terrace-cogitant.label: Third-Terrace Cogitant
facts: [d1] Found alive on the third-terrace floor — seated, chalk-covered from hem to shoulder, arm moving in ceaseless four-second repetition across a low board. Pallet unused and dust-covered. Cup empty and dr

[d1] Order-Sense partial read: the notation is a single continuous structured calculation, four days old, built outward from a fixed coordinate — a precise location somewhere in the valley. Ossian stepped in and named it: 'She was working on the source.' The exact location slipped past the partial read.

[d1] A direct quiet word — crouching to her level, one syllable — stopped her arm for three seconds. Her face loosened. She did not turn. She resumed. She is reachable, barely, and she is not done with the calculation.

[d1] Cold to the touch — shaded-stone cold, not critical. Blinked once when the bedroll settled across her shoulders. Body-awareness faint but present. Cup refilled and within reach. The chalk columns are nearly at the right margin.

[d1] Surfaced from the four-day calculation trance the moment the notation was complete. First words: 'You're sitting on the floor.' Her focus arrived in stages. She is present, fragile, and has not yet spoken the coordinate aloud.

ferris.label: Ferris
facts: [d1] Lenten's replacement runner for Brenn — broad-shouldered, young, now briefed on the Cogitarium's particular needs. Was told about Deren. Knows the dry-stone wall-path hazard.

maren-soule.label: Maren Soule
facts: [d1] A woman who kept a warden post on the old ridge road before the Transition. Was present at a remediation site closure — direct exposure to active override vocabulary. Did not leave the valley. Lives somewhere in the middle valley now; exact location unknown even to Ossian. May still carry working…

warden-archive-radiant-plateau.label: Warden Archive (Radiant Plateau)
facts: [d1] The Warden Archive at the Radiant Plateau held more complete Transition-era records than any settlement. Whether the archive survived intact and whether anyone there can still read it is unconfirmed — Ossian has not sent anyone to ask, which he acknowledges as a gap.

lenten.name: Lenten
lenten.role: Builder at Waymarker — the one who made the Cogitarium's supply line work, and who knows exactly what it cost to make it work
lenten.voiceHints: Speaks in load-bearing sentences — nothing decorative, nothing wasted, but not cold. Measures what he says the way he measures timber: cut once, cut right. Has a builder's habit of naming the thing underneath the thing — the joist behind the wall, the assumption behind the proposal. Goes quiet when he's calculating, and the quiet feels occupied, not absent. Early-spring mud still on his boots.
knowledge: The Cogitarium supply line in full — every dependency, every seasonal choke point, every person who makes it hold
Which parts of the evacuation proposal would snap the line immediately, and which would fray it slow
What Waymarker's builders actually believe, as opposed to what they've been willing to say out loud
That abstract plans have a weight and it falls on specific people — he has watched it fall
lenten.wants: For the decision — whatever it turns out to be — to be made by people who have walked the route and felt the cold, not only by people who have thought about it well.
lenten.fears: That the evacuation debate will be resolved at the level of argument before anyone has checked what argument costs on the ground, and that he will have been too careful to say so clearly enough.

reactsToReputation.practical: opens up, visibly; offers the real numbers
reactsToReputation.theoretical: listens with full patience, then asks the one grounding question that hasn't been asked yet
reactsToReputation.trustworthy: takes longer to decide than you'd expect, because he weighs it, but once decided it holds

lenten.currentState: Sitting in his shed at Waymarker in the thin early-spring light, with a logbook open and a concern he hasn't voiced yet sitting beside it like a second presence in the room.
lenten.roleMethod: Cogitarium method — sustains the system by mapping its true shape, reveals the hidden load-bearing truth before the structure is tested by weight it was never designed to carry

provenance.hint: Lenten — a builder at Waymarker who arranged the Cogitarium supply line; practical, settled, probably cautious about the
provenance.why: Saehara has arrived at Lenten's shed and is about to make contact — Lenten needs a stable, persistent identity

offscreen.note: Lenten spent part of the evening re-walking a section of the coldest stretch of the supply route alone, muttering figures under his breath — not to recalculate anything, but to remind himself what the numbers actually feel like when the wind is in your face.

Lenten spent the evening tallying last season's spoilage figures by hand, making a clean copy to press into the hands of whoever calls the next planning meeting — evidence that the route's cost speaks for itself, in numbers no one sat at a desk produced.

Lenten spent the evening re-marking her worn route maps with fresh charcoal, underlining the three stretches where cold and load together broke two carriers last winter — her quiet way of preparing evidence no committee meeting can ignore.

gen-cogitarium-third-terrace.name: Cogitarium Third Terrace
gen-cogitarium-third-terrace.descriptionSeed: A place the road led to — Cogitarium Third Terrace. The fiction brought you here before the map knew its name.

establishedFacts.text: A Cogitant collapsed in the entrance hall — the third this week. Ossian noted it without alarm. The Cogitarium's bodies are failing at an accelerating rate.
Saehara has taken the fallen Cogitant from the Cogitarium and is carrying her northeast toward Waymarker — she cannot be left behind; her two-to-three day window is now the mission's pace.
Lenten has committed to dispatching a supply runner to the Cogitarium before evening — the supply line is formally reopening tonight.
The third-terrace Cogitant is still at the chalk board — arm in four-second repetition, new notation added, cup empty and dry. Saehara is returning to reach her.
The third-terrace Cogitant's most immediate physical needs have been answered — water cup filled and set within reach, bedroll laid across her shoulders. She is cold but not critically so.
The fallen Cogitant's name is Deren — confirmed by Ossian.
The Cogitarium supply line is now operational — Ferris is the new runner, the arrangement explained, food distributed to the side table. The line will hold as long as Ferris does.
Ossian has eaten — reached for bread of his own motion after accepting that tending the body is calculably correct. The body is not yet abandoned.
